# OpenDDIL HQ — llms.txt
# https://llmstxt.org/

> OpenDDIL HQ is the central event processor for the OpenDDIL framework.
> It uses Restate Virtual Objects to durably consume events from Redpanda
> and apply them to PostgreSQL with exactly-once guarantees.
> Built entirely in Python, demonstrating the polyglot architecture
> (C# Edge clients → Protobuf → Redpanda → Python HQ processor).

## Project Purpose
Backend processor for DDIL environments. Consumes Protobuf CloudEvent bytes from
Redpanda, deserializes them, and applies domain logic to PostgreSQL with durable execution.

## Architecture
- Restate Virtual Object "InventoryProcessor" keyed by item_id
- Single-writer concurrency per item (concurrent allocations queued automatically)
- ctx.run() wraps every DB side effect for durable journaling
- On crash replay, journaled operations are NOT re-executed (exactly-once)
- Three outcomes: valid allocation, insufficient stock (compensating audit), item not found

## Key Files
- src/openddil_hq/inventory_handler.py: InventoryProcessor Virtual Object handler.
- src/openddil_hq/__init__.py: Package init.

## Dependencies
- restate-sdk: Official Restate Python SDK.
- asyncpg: Async PostgreSQL driver.
- protobuf: Protobuf deserialization.
- hypercorn: ASGI server.
- Generated Python classes from openddil-contracts (make python).

## Durable Execution Pattern
- ALL database operations MUST be wrapped in ctx.run().
- ctx.run("name", async_function) — name identifies the journal entry.
- Inventory update + audit log are in a SINGLE ctx.run() with a DB transaction.
- On replay: returns the journaled result WITHOUT re-executing the function.

## Running
- Serve: python -m hypercorn openddil_hq.inventory_handler:app --bind 0.0.0.0:9080
- Register: POST http://localhost:9070/deployments {"uri": "http://host:9080"}
